question: When a feature is in a non-terminal phase but its gate has failed (gate_blocked / recirculated), which column should its card appear in on the Kanban board?
options: Current phase column, with a distinct visual marker (e.g. red/amber badge) to indicate the blocked state
A dedicated 'Blocked' column appended after Delivery
Current phase column with no special treatment — status badge on the card is sufficient
Other

question: How should the Kanban board handle features whose status is 'draft' (not yet started, no current phase)?
options: Place draft features in a 'Backlog' column shown before Inception
Place draft features in the Inception column
Exclude draft features from the Kanban board entirely
Other

question: How should the board handle features that have completed the final phase (Delivery done) — should they appear in the Delivery column or be filtered out by default?
options: Completed features appear in the Delivery column with a 'Done' visual treatment
Completed features are hidden by default, with a toggle to show them
Completed features appear in the Delivery column with no special treatment
Other

question: Should the user be able to move cards between columns by dragging (interactive state change), or is the board read-only with navigation handled by clicking through to the detail page?
options: Read-only board — cards navigate to detail page on click, phase changes happen via existing detail-page controls
Drag-and-drop to change phase, persisted via the existing advance/recirculate API
Other

question: Should the Kanban board render all six phase columns (Inception, Planning, Construction, Review, Testing, Delivery) on screen at once, or adapt the number of visible columns to viewport width?
options: All six columns always rendered, horizontal scroll when viewport is too narrow
All six columns always rendered, each column shrinks to fit the viewport (cards may be narrow)
Render as many columns as fit the viewport, horizontal scroll for the rest
Other

question: When there are many features in a single phase column, how should the column behave?
options: Render all cards in the column (no virtualization, no limit) — relies on CSS scroll within the column
Render all cards in the column with a sticky column header so the phase label stays visible while scrolling
Show a configurable maximum (e.g. 20) with a 'Show N more' expander at the bottom of the column
Other

question: Should the Kanban view preserve the existing sort controls from the list view (by phase / priority / status / updated), or does the Kanban layout make those redundant?
options: Cards within each column are sorted by priority (P1 first) then by last-updated descending; no sort controls shown
Keep the existing sort controls above the board; they reorder cards within each column
Cards within each column are sorted by last-updated descending only; no sort controls shown
Other
